title: Unpacked Countdown, a Gemini Rebrand, and Apple vs OpenAI
intro: Samsung's Galaxy Unpacked is three days out, Google quietly folded NotebookLM into Gemini, and Apple just took OpenAI to court. Plus a one-click Mac audio trick and an honest mid-2026 AI showdown.

posts.body: Samsung's Galaxy Unpacked is officially set for July 22 in London, and leaks point to five devices — refreshed Fold and Flip foldables plus a rumored mystery fifth device. It streams live on Samsung.com and YouTube at 9AM ET / 6:30PM IST. The real question isn't specs, it's whether this is the year foldables finally stop being a flex and start being the default. Leak details are still rumor until Samsung says otherwise; the date and event are confirmed.
posts.problem: With Unpacked three days away, the gap between confirmed facts (the date) and rumored specifics (the actual device lineup) is at its widest — easy to mistake leaks for announcements.
posts.breakthrough: Galaxy Unpacked is officially set for July 22 in London, streaming live on Samsung.com and YouTube at 9AM ET / 6:30PM IST, with leaks pointing to five devices — refreshed Fold and Flip foldables plus a rumored mystery fifth device.
posts.catch: The date and event are confirmed; the device lineup, including that mystery fifth device, is still leak-based until Samsung actually says otherwise on stage.
posts.forYou: Set a reminder for the livestream rather than trusting the leak roundups — the real question worth watching for isn't the spec sheet, it's whether this is the year foldables stop being a flex and become the default.

posts.body: Google has renamed NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook, folding one of its most genuinely useful research tools fully under the Gemini brand. Same idea — drop your sources, get grounded answers and audio overviews — now with clearer Gemini integration. The tool itself is still one of the best ways to actually reason over your own documents instead of trusting a model's memory.
posts.problem: Google has been quietly consolidating its AI tools under a single brand, and NotebookLM — one of its most genuinely useful research tools — remained a separate, less-integrated product.
posts.breakthrough: Google renamed NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook, folding it fully under the Gemini brand with clearer integration, while keeping the core idea intact: drop your sources in and get grounded answers and audio overviews back.
posts.catch: A rebrand doesn't change the underlying tool's capabilities — anyone expecting new features from the name change alone will be looking at the same product with a new label.
posts.forYou: If you haven't used NotebookLM before, look for it under its new name, Gemini Notebook — it's still one of the best ways to make an AI reason over your own documents instead of trusting its memory.

posts.hook: Switch your Mac's audio device in one click.
posts.body: Hold Option and click the Sound icon in your Mac's menu bar — you get an instant switcher for every audio output and input device, no diving into System Settings. Jumping between AirPods, speakers, and a headset becomes a single click. Small habit, saves you the settings detour every single day.
posts.problem: Switching audio output on a Mac — between AirPods, speakers, and a headset — usually means digging into System Settings every single time.
posts.breakthrough: Holding Option and clicking the Sound icon in the Mac menu bar brings up an instant switcher for every audio output and input device, letting you jump between devices in a single click.
posts.catch: It's an Option-click shortcut that's easy to forget exists if you've never stumbled onto it — there's no visual hint in the menu bar that holding Option changes anything.
posts.forYou: Try it right now: hold Option and click your Mac's Sound icon — if you switch audio devices even occasionally, this replaces a settings detour with one click, every day.

posts.hook: ChatGPT vs Gemini vs Claude — the mid-2026 verdict.
posts.body: There's no single winner anymore, so pick per job. ChatGPT (GPT-5.6) is the best all-rounder and still owns voice. Gemini 2.5 Deep Think is the research beast with a 2M-token context window for long documents. Claude Fable 5 is the coding king at ~80% on SWE-Bench. Everyday use and voice lean ChatGPT, deep research leans Gemini, and coding or agents lean Claude. Match the tool to the task instead of chasing one app to rule them all.
posts.problem: Picking a single 'best' AI assistant means implicitly accepting worse performance on whatever task that model isn't built for — and the three leading models now specialize in genuinely different things.
posts.breakthrough: The mid-2026 verdict: ChatGPT (GPT-5.6) is the best all-rounder and still owns voice, Gemini 2.5 Deep Think is the research beast with a 2M-token context window for long documents, and Claude Fable 5 is the coding king at roughly 80% on SWE-Bench.
posts.catch: These specialties shift as each lab ships updates — this is a snapshot of mid-2026, not a permanent hierarchy, and Gemini 3.5 Pro's eventual release could reshuffle the research and context-window lead specifically.
posts.forYou: Match the tool to the job instead of picking one to be loyal to: ChatGPT for everyday use and voice, Gemini for deep research and long documents, Claude for coding or agents.

posts.hook: Apple just sued OpenAI — and it says a lot.
posts.body: Apple has filed suit against OpenAI alleging theft of trade secrets. Whatever the legal merits, the subtext is loud: the friendly AI-partnership era is over, and the platform owners now see the model labs as direct rivals. When the company that once shipped ChatGPT into its OS is reaching for lawyers, you know the AI power map is being redrawn in real time.
posts.problem: Two years ago, Apple shipped ChatGPT directly into its own operating system — a level of trust between a platform owner and a model lab that looked like the start of a durable partnership.
posts.breakthrough: Apple has now filed suit against OpenAI alleging theft of trade secrets — the same company it once integrated directly into iOS is now facing it in court.
posts.catch: The legal merits of the case are still unproven, but the symbolic shift has already happened regardless of how the lawsuit resolves — that's the part that isn't in dispute.
posts.forYou: Read this less as 'who's right' and more as a marker of a new era: platform owners now see model labs as direct competitors, not partners, and that will shape which AI features show up on your devices going forward.
